name: Logseq
description: A privacy-first, open-source knowledge base that works on top of local plain-text Markdown and Org-mode files.

name: Joplin
description: An open-source note-taking and to-do application, which can handle a large number of notes organized into notebooks.

name: Zettlr
description: A Markdown editor aimed at academics and researchers, featuring Zettelkasten support and citation management.

name: QOwnNotes
description: A plain-text file notepad and todo-list manager with Markdown support and Nextcloud integration.

name: Standard Notes
description: A simple and private notes app that features end-to-end encryption.

name: Anytype
description: A local-first, peer-to-peer personal knowledge base and productivity app built on a graph structure.

name: Gnote
description: A desktop note-taking application for Linux, ported from Tomboy to C++.

name: Tomboy
description: A desktop note-taking app using a wiki-style linking system.

name: CherryTree
description: A hierarchical note-taking application, featuring rich text formatting and syntax highlighting.

name: Trilium Notes
description: A hierarchical note-taking application with a focus on building large personal knowledge bases.

name: Zim Wiki
description: A graphical text editor used to maintain a collection of locally stored wiki pages.

name: Obsidian
description: A powerful knowledge base that works on top of a local folder of plain text Markdown files. (Note: Free but proprietary, with open ecosystem).

name: Org-mode
description: A comprehensive document formatting, organizing, and maintaining system integrated within the Emacs editor.

name: Foam
description: A personal knowledge management and sharing system inspired by Roam Research, built as an extension for VS Code.

name: Markor
description: A lightweight Markdown and todo.txt text editor specifically designed for Android.

name: NotallyX
description: A minimalistic, visually appealing, open-source note-taking app for Android.

name: SilentNotes
description: A privacy-focused note-taking app that supports end-to-end encryption and syncs between devices.

name: Quillpad
description: A beautifully designed note-taking app for Android, heavily inspired by Google Keep.

name: KeePassXC
description: A community-driven, cross-platform port of the Windows application 'KeePass Password Safe'.

name: KeePassDX
description: An open-source password manager for Android that is fully compatible with KeePass files.

name: Bitwarden
description: A popular, open-source password management solution that syncs across all devices via the cloud or self-hosting.

name: VeraCrypt
description: A free, open-source disk encryption software for Windows, Mac OSX, and Linux.

name: Cryptomator
description: Free client-side encryption tailored for protecting files stored in public cloud services.

name: OpenKeychain
description: An OpenPGP implementation for Android, allowing the encryption and decryption of emails and files.

name: GnuPG
description: A complete, free command-line implementation of the OpenPGP standard for secure communication.

name: OnionShare
description: An open-source tool that securely and anonymously shares files, hosts websites, and chats using the Tor network.

name: Tails OS
description: A portable, live operating system designed to protect against surveillance and censorship, routing traffic through Tor.

name: DroidStealth
description: An open-source Android application aimed at managing permissions and enhancing overall user privacy.

name: Orbot
description: A free proxy app that empowers other mobile apps to use the internet more securely via the Tor network.

name: Tor Browser
description: A heavily modified web browser that anonymizes web traffic by routing it through the decentralized Tor network.

name: MAT2
description: A command-line metadata removal tool supporting a wide array of commonly used image, audio, and document formats.

name: BleachBit
description: A free cache cleaner, privacy guard, and system optimizer designed to free up disk space.

name: SecureDrop Workstation
description: An open-source whistleblower submission system that media organizations use to securely accept documents.

name: ExifTool
description: A comprehensive command-line application for reading, writing, and editing meta information across various file types.

name: Etherpad
description: A highly customizable open-source online editor providing collaborative, real-time rich-text editing.

name: CryptPad
description: A private-by-design, end-to-end encrypted alternative to popular office tools and collaborative cloud services.

name: Nextcloud Client
description: Desktop and mobile clients used to interface with Nextcloud, a self-hosted, open-source cloud productivity platform.

name: Syncthing
description: A continuous file synchronization program that securely syncs files between two or more devices in real-time without a central server.

name: Delta Chat
description: A chat app that works like traditional messengers but operates over the existing, decentralized email server network.

name: Matrix
description: An open standard and protocol for secure, decentralized, real-time communication and chat.

name: Jitsi Meet
description: A set of open-source projects that allows you to easily build and deploy secure video conferencing solutions without requiring accounts.

name: Mumble
description: A free, open-source, low-latency, and high-quality voice chat application primarily intended for gamers and teams.

name: Rocket.Chat
description: An open-source, fully customizable enterprise communications platform designed as a Slack alternative.

name: Zulip
description: An open-source team chat app that uniquely organizes conversations using topic-based threading.

name: Mattermost
description: An open-source, self-hostable online chat service offering messaging, file sharing, search, and deep integrations.

name: Tox
description: A decentralized, peer-to-peer instant-messaging and video-calling protocol that offers end-to-end encryption without central servers.

name: Briar
description: A messaging app designed for activists and journalists that syncs via Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, or Tor, bypassing central servers entirely.

name: Session Messenger
description: An end-to-end encrypted messenger that minimizes metadata collection and routes messages through an onion-routing network.
